At any time in the proôÙcess, ideas, sugôÙgesôÙtions, and criôÙtiôÙcism from anyôÙbody in the hierôÙarchy is welôÙcome and is serôÙiôÙously conôÙsidered. Your ideas are welôÙcome, even if they mean that we have to change a lot of code to make things better.

We have published sevôÙeral sciôÙentific artôÙicles in the areas of ArtiôÙfiôÙcial IntelôÙliôÙgence and ComôÙputer GraphôÙics and we will encourage you to do the same. We sponôÙsor visôÙits to conôÙferôÙences and have close relaôÙtionôÙships with uniôÙversitôÙies and research instiôÙtutes in the U.S. and GerôÙmany.

think-cell encourôÙages a healthy work-life balôÙance. We do not work at night or on weekôÙends.

We pay very comôÙpetôÙitôÙive salarôÙies and offer our developers EUR 130,000 annuôÙally folôÙlowôÙing one year of employôÙment. If necessary, we will go out of our way to help you reloôÙcate to BerôÙlin and will do what we can to help you acquire a work perôÙmit. While you should be able to comôÙmuôÙnicôÙate effectively in English, knowôÙing GerôÙman is not required. GerôÙman lanôÙguage courses can be arranged if desired.

## What You Need to Be Successful _(Skills)_

### Must Have
- C++ 
- Algorithms


### Relevant Experiences
In fact, we have several engineers who joined right after graduating from university. 

## About Our Company
think-cell is a fast-paced software company in Berlin, Germany, with a focus on developing graphics products that stand out from the crowd. More than 1,000,000 users worldwide rely on [our software](https://www.think-cell.com/en/product) for their daily business as it makes creating graphical presentations so much easier, faster, and more enjoyable. Among our customers are many renowned consulting companies and large international corporations.

We do not have to make compromises with regard to code quality and beauty, because think-cell is highly profitable. We are willing to go the extra mile to develop sophisticated algorithms and refine our user interface, and we are proud of our many happy customers. The fact that the company is owned and managed by seasoned computer scientists certainly contributes to a working environment that makes exceptional developers come and stay.

### About our softôÙware

Our focus is on busiôÙness slides (as opposed to more artôÙful applicôÙaôÙtions) because they offer great potential for autoôÙmaôÙtion of layôÙout tasks that are traôÙdiôÙtionôÙally perôÙformed by PowerôÙPoint users themselves. ChalôÙlenges are plenty: from a solid underôÙstandôÙing of what makes a good layôÙout and which guidelines are folôÙlowed by humans who do manual layôÙout, to algorithms that proôÙduce an acceptôÙable outôÙput fast enough for interôÙactôÙive slide design, to a graphôÙical user interôÙface that supôÙports our new, oriôÙginal approach to slide layôÙout in a way that is easy to underôÙstand yet unobôÙtrusôÙive, to solid techôÙnical soluôÙtions for autoôÙmatic bug reportôÙing and autoôÙmatic updates, to comôÙpatôÙibôÙilôÙity with third-party softôÙware on the comôÙputers of half a milôÙlion users.

Here are some highlights of what we have done.

**LanôÙguage**

- Everything we do is C++. Even our customer portal is written in C++. There is some AssemôÙbler glue code where it is necessary, and our build scripts are written in Python, but other than that think-cell is all about C++.
- We closely track the latest verôÙsions of our comôÙpilers, Visual C++ and Xcode, so we can always use the latest C++ standôÙard feaôÙtures as soon as they become availôÙable.
- We fund the workôÙing group for proôÙgramôÙming lanôÙguages of the GerôÙman InstiôÙtute for StandôÙardôÙizôÙaôÙtion (DIN). Some of our employôÙees are memôÙbers of this comôÙmitôÙtee and vote in the interôÙnaôÙtional standôÙardôÙizôÙaôÙtion process of ISO/IEC C++.
- We sponôÙsor the StandôÙard C++ FoundôÙaôÙtion helpôÙing them to proôÙmote the underôÙstandôÙing and use of modôÙern StandôÙard C++ on all comôÙpilers and platôÙforms.

**LibôÙrary**

- We use Boost throughout our code, e.g., BoostãSpirit for parsôÙing.
- We have our own range libôÙrary, in the same spirit as Boost. Range or Eric Nieblerãs range-v3, but going furôÙther, for example, by uniôÙfyôÙing internal and external iterôÙaôÙtion. We gave a talk about it, and most of the code is pubôÙlic.
- We develop our own cross-platôÙform libôÙrary to supôÙport Mac and WinôÙdows with a single code base.
- We have our own refôÙerôÙence-countôÙing and perôÙsistôÙence libôÙrarôÙies to save and restore whole object trees.
- We have an extensôÙive bug reportôÙing infraôÙstrucôÙture. AsserôÙtions and error checks stay in the release code, and our softôÙware autoôÙmatôÙicôÙally reports bugs to our server. The server anaôÙlyzes the bug, catôÙegorôÙizes it, and files it in a dataôÙbase that all developers can access. If an update fixes the bug, the user can downôÙload the update directly from a bug response web page.

**Algorithms**

- think-cell was founôÙded on the idea for an algorithm for autoôÙmatic slide layôÙout, and we are still on an excitôÙing jourôÙney towards that ambiôÙtious visôÙion. You can see our most recent release in action!
- We developed a new algorithm for autoôÙmatic point cloud labeling that allows labels to be posiôÙtioned away from the actual points.
- We developed a new algorithm for autoôÙmatic column chart labeling.
- We are workôÙing with John ForôÙrest ã author of the linôÙear solver CLP ã to make his simôÙplex code faster on our kind of probôÙlems.
- We developed many genôÙeric data strucôÙtures that are not in C++ or Boost, for example parôÙtiôÙtions.
- Our software not only produces charts, it is also able to read them back from paper. For our chart recogôÙniôÙtion tool, we rely on OpenCV and the LeptonôÙica Image ProôÙcessôÙing LibôÙrary.

**Reverse EnginôÙeerôÙing**

- We do lots of reverse enginôÙeerôÙing with the disôÙasôÙsemôÙbler IDA from Hex-Rays, in order to achieve things that are not posôÙsible via the docôÙuôÙmenôÙted Microsoft Office API.
- We wrote probôÙably the best funcôÙtion hookôÙing engine out there. At each start of our softôÙware, we patch the Microsoft Office executôÙables in memory. Rather than hard-codôÙing patch addresses, we search for small chunks of assembly code to be robust against minor changes in the executôÙables.

## How to apply

Please apply via this link: [Click here to apply](https://www.think-cell.com/career/jobs/development?ec=rC0IZep7h7pmLcx8ndavN6CYqAqwS1w6rWJ8C30Q0MQcBTL97qhrOxn34Sklr6jQNODy_edu-fd-bsHwp3MXAOL7NPAAMb1hHS7_WMeGegscny4d4DTH5RQfQqnnuy5IN6MX6_I)

After you apply through the link, our team will reach out to you through e-mail.
If you have any questions, please contact our team via e-mail: hr@think-cell.com

We are a venture capital-backed company with co-founders who are experienced startup veterans, having successfully led previous companies to acquisition. You will have the unique opportunity to shape the future of our trading & social media platform with high-traffic and contribute to its success. - Conduct thorough testing and debugging to identify and resolve issues promptly.

- Bachelor's degree in Computer Science or a related field.
- 4+ years of hands-on experience in backend development with Node.js

- Experience with RESTful API and WebSocket.
- Experience with MySQL, MongoDB

- Experience with **social media platforms, live streaming** or related technologies is a plus.

- 10Ò˜ - 15Ò˜ NTD / month.

# TûÙtulo del trabajo

Desarrollador full stack con experiencia en visualizaciû°n de datos usando librerûÙas JavaScript.

## Sobre Codeando Mûˋxico

Codeando Mûˋxico es una comunidad de hackers cûÙvicos que busca fortalecer el trabajo e impacto de personas, equipos e instituciones inciden en el espacio pû¤blico con proyectos de tecnologûÙa y datos.

Cualquiera que comparta nuestros principios, valores y cû°digo de conducta puede ser parte de esta comunidad.

## Sobre el proyecto

El proyecto EcoZonas se centra en la implementaciû°n de proyectos piloto en dos barrios vulnerables de ciudades mexicanas de tamaûÝo medio con el objetivo de desarrollar, apoyar y fortalecer procesos y mûˋtodos participativos de acciû°n climûÀtica inclusiva y replicable a nivel comunitario, enmarcados en planes de acciû°n climûÀtica municipales y nacionales. Los principales beneficiarios son las comunidades locales, sociedad civil organizada, los municipios y los gobiernos estatales y nacionales.

Como parte del proyecto EcoZonas se van a realizar dos procesos de recopilaciû°n de datos:
* Una encuesta
* Un mapeo comunitario

A partir de los datos recopilados durante la encuesta y el mapeo comunitario se generarûÀ un reporte web y un reporte en PDF.

## Sobre el puesto

Estamos buscando a una persona que le interese el uso de la tecnologûÙa y los datos como herramientas para resolver problemas pû¤blicos.

EspecûÙficamente, un desarrollador full stack, acostumbrado al trabajo remoto y la colaboraciû°n asûÙncrona, con experiencia en desarrollo de aplicaciones web, con el aûÝadido de tener conocimiento sobre librerûÙas de visualizaciû°n de datos JavaScript y con algo de experiencia implementando dashboards y/o visualizaciones.

La vacante de desarrollador full stack con conocimientos de visualizaciû°n de datos es para la implementaciû°n del reporte web y el reporte en PDF. El reporte web consistirûÀ en la creaciû°n de un documento dinûÀmico, dependiendo del resultado del mapeo, que contendrûÀ diversas visualizaciones.

## Responsabilidades

1. Participar en un equipo para la construcciû°n de un producto a partir de sus especificaciones, participando en la definiciû°n de las tecnologûÙas o librerûÙas a usar para la visualizaciû°n de datos.

2. En este caso el producto consiste en el desarrollo de un reporte web y un reporte descargable en PDF, generado de manera dinûÀmica a partir de los datos recopilados en la encuesta y en el mapeo comunitario.

3. Implementar flujos de usuario completos a partir de historias de usuario, desde la consulta de datos, procesamiento de los mismos y terminando en la generaciû°n de visualizaciones en el front end.

4. Implementar un diseûÝo de interfaz de usuario de manera modular, ya sea usando vanilla JavaScript o frameworks UI (React, Vue, Svelte, Web Components). Estamos abiertos a propuestas sobre las librerûÙas a usar.

## Lo que buscamos

1. Experiencia en desarrollo front end.
2. Experiencia implementando visualizaciones de datos con el apoyo de librerûÙas JavaScript (Highcharts, Plotly, Vega) o en caso de ser necesario desarrollo ad-hoc de visualizaciones con D3.
3. Experiencia en el desarrollo backend. De preferencia con python (Flask, Django, etc), pero estamos abiertos a otras tecnologûÙas.
4. El proyecto estûÀ pensado para una carga de trabajo de media jornada durante un mes. Priorizamos resultados antes que horarios.

* ÞÂ Remoto, aunque es un plus si estûÀs radicado en Ciudad de Mûˋxico por si fuera necesario alguna reuniû°n presencial.

* Þ Fecha de inicio tercera semana de octubre de 2023
* Þ¡ Remuneraciû°n: $30,000 + IVA
